  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resources Officer",
  "description": "New Incentives&nbsp;is an evidence-based organization that aims to provide incentives to increase childhood vaccination rates and save lives in the most cost-effective way. While caregivers and infants remain our top priority, our operations are guided by accountability, integrity, and a commitment to excellence.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Human Resources Officer

Job Location: Bauchi
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Human Resources Officer will be assisting the HR Manager in recruitment, employee relations management, staff benefits administration, staff compensation, staff performance management, and training.
	The ideal candidate should be someone with at least 3 years experience in Human Resources, very diligent, detailed, and process oriented with some understanding of legal and logistics processes.
	The candidate should be residing in North West Nigeria. Excellent communication skills, a learning mindset, and ability to adapt quickly managing HR systems are essential to succeed in this role.


Job Responsibilities and Tasks
Recruitment (30%):


	Assist in FOs selection and in-person interviews at the LGAs level.
	Take the lead to manage in-person interviews of shortlisted FOs candidates through local New Incentives team members.
	Escalate any gaps, challenges, or noncompliance to the HR Manager for immediate follow-up.
	Liaise with the SR unit and community leaders to ensure locals are recruited.
	Promote job ads on social media and do outreaches on platforms like LinkedIn and your network to identify candidates.
	Assist the HR Manager in sending invitations, following up with candidates through phone calls, scheduling interviews, and conducting reference and background checks.
	Send feedback for the concluded recruitment exercise.
	Prepare the recruitment Gsheet during and after the process.


Onboarding, Benefits and Compensation Management (40%):


	Follow up with the new employees to ensure pension and NHF registration are done, and numbers are generated.
	Follow up with the new office account opening during onboarding.
	Liaise with PFAs, NHF, NSITF, and tax offices to follow up on remittances.
	Physical Training sessions for new hires in the state.


Administration (30%):


	Ensure employee information is up to date.
	Review employee leave applications on Zoho and ensure the proper documents are uploaded.
	Any other duty that the HR Manager or console may assign.


Education and Work Experience Requirements


	BSc or BA in Business Administration, Social Sciences or its recognized equivalent
	At least 2 - 5 years in a Human Resources general function.
	1 - 2 years prior experience working for a non-profit, charitable organization, NGO or (I)NGO is a plus
	Priority will be given to candidates residing in the states where the position is advertised.


Skills and Competencies Requirement:


	Preferably a candidate already based in Bauchi State.
	Detail-oriented and diligent professional
	Process-oriented with some understanding of legal and logistics processes
	Good networks and contacts in North West Nigeria and ability to leverage for recruitment
	Good organizational skills, effective time management, ability to meet deadlines, multi-task, and prioritize tasks
	Sound cultural awareness and ability to work in multicultural and multiethnic environments
	Proven managerial and/or leadership skills
	Ability to deliver high-quality work in short periods and to work under pressure.
	Critical thinker and possess a problem-solving attitude
	Passion to help others and reduce infant mortality
	Very good communication and writing skills, English, Hausa and dialects (verbal and written)
	Excellent responsiveness to email and phone requests
	Proficiency in MS Word, MS Excel, MS PowerPoint, internet browsers, smartphones, and ICT in general. The candidate should be willing to use Google Docs, Google Sheets, and Google Slides.
	Motivation to work in a young organization that is constantly changing based on stakeholder feedback and operates with a lean structure.


Language Requirement:


	Proficiency in English Language (conversation, reading and writing) is required
	Knowledge of Hausa, Nupe and/or other commonly spoken Languages in target locations (conversation and reading) is an advantage.


Note


	Candidates will be considered on a rolling basis and are encouraged not to wait until the deadline, as decisions for this position could be made sooner.
	DISCLAIMER: Beware of fraudulent job offers. New Incentives &ndash; All Babies Are Equal Initiative does not charge a fee at any stage of the recruitment process.
	Speaking local languages in the states where we are operating would be an added advantage.
	Women and persons with disabilities are strongly encouraged to apply for this position!
	All internal applications and employee are strongly advised to use their official email address while applying.

Human Resources Officer Job at New Incentives




        
    

        
        New Incentives is an evidence-based organization that aims to provide incentives to increase childhood vaccination rates and save lives in the most cost-effective way. While caregivers and infants remain our top priority, our operations are guided by accountability, integrity, and a commitment to excellence.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources Officer

Job Location: Bauchi
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary

	The Human Resources Officer will be assisting the HR Manager in recruitment, employee relations management, staff benefits administration, staff compensation, staff performance management, and training.
	The ideal candidate should be someone with at least 3 years experience in Human Resources, very diligent, detailed, and process oriented with some understanding of legal and logistics processes.
	The candidate should be residing in North West Nigeria. Excellent communication skills, a learning mindset, and ability to adapt quickly managing HR systems are essential to succeed in this role.


Job Responsibilities and Tasks
Recruitment (30%):


	Assist in FOs selection and in-person interviews at the LGAs level.
	Take the lead to manage in-person interviews of shortlisted FOs candidates through local New Incentives team members.
	Escalate any gaps, challenges, or noncompliance to the HR Manager for immediate follow-up.
	Liaise with the SR unit and community leaders to ensure locals are recruited.
	Promote job ads on social media and do outreaches on platforms like LinkedIn and your network to identify candidates.
	Assist the HR Manager in sending invitations, following up with candidates through phone calls, scheduling interviews, and conducting reference and background checks.
	Send feedback for the concluded recruitment exercise.
	Prepare the recruitment Gsheet during and after the process.


Onboarding, Benefits and Compensation Management (40%):


	Follow up with the new employees to ensure pension and NHF registration are done, and numbers are generated.
	Follow up with the new office account opening during onboarding.
	Liaise with PFAs, NHF, NSITF, and tax offices to follow up on remittances.
	Physical Training sessions for new hires in the state.


Administration (30%):


	Ensure employee information is up to date.
	Review employee leave applications on Zoho and ensure the proper documents are uploaded.
	Any other duty that the HR Manager or console may assign.


Education and Work Experience Requirements


	BSc or BA in Business Administration, Social Sciences or its recognized equivalent
	At least 2 - 5 years in a Human Resources general function.
	1 - 2 years prior experience working for a non-profit, charitable organization, NGO or (I)NGO is a plus
	Priority will be given to candidates residing in the states where the position is advertised.


Skills and Competencies Requirement:


	Preferably a candidate already based in Bauchi State.
	Detail-oriented and diligent professional
	Process-oriented with some understanding of legal and logistics processes
	Good networks and contacts in North West Nigeria and ability to leverage for recruitment
	Good organizational skills, effective time management, ability to meet deadlines, multi-task, and prioritize tasks
	Sound cultural awareness and ability to work in multicultural and multiethnic environments
	Proven managerial and/or leadership skills
	Ability to deliver high-quality work in short periods and to work under pressure.
	Critical thinker and possess a problem-solving attitude
	Passion to help others and reduce infant mortality
	Very good communication and writing skills, English, Hausa and dialects (verbal and written)
	Excellent responsiveness to email and phone requests
	Proficiency in MS Word, MS Excel, MS PowerPoint, internet browsers, smartphones, and ICT in general. The candidate should be willing to use Google Docs, Google Sheets, and Google Slides.
	Motivation to work in a young organization that is constantly changing based on stakeholder feedback and operates with a lean structure.


Language Requirement:


	Proficiency in English Language (conversation, reading and writing) is required
	Knowledge of Hausa, Nupe and/or other commonly spoken Languages in target locations (conversation and reading) is an advantage.


Note


	Candidates will be considered on a rolling basis and are encouraged not to wait until the deadline, as decisions for this position could be made sooner.
	DISCLAIMER: Beware of fraudulent job offers. New Incentives – All Babies Are Equal Initiative does not charge a fee at any stage of the recruitment process.
	Speaking local languages in the states where we are operating would be an added advantage.
	Women and persons with disabilities are strongly encouraged to apply for this position!
	All internal applications and employee are strongly advised to use their official email address while applying.

  "description": "Rise Networks is Nigeria&rsquo;s pioneering AI for Development (AI4D) organization, building Africa&rsquo;s next generation of ethical AI talent and innovators. Through research, training, advisory, and policy engagement, we equip individuals and organizations with future &ndash; ready skills that bridge learning and employment. Our work advances Tech Justice and Digital Rights, promotes inclusive digital literacy, and drives policy influence and community transformation. By doing so, Rise Networks positions Africa as a co-creator in the global AI ecosystem, shaping an ethical, innovation-driven future.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Communications &amp; Digital Strategy Analyst

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time&nbsp;

About The Role


	Communications &amp; Digital Strategy Analyst will design and execute integrated, high-impact communications strategies that strengthen RISE&#39; reputation, deepen stakeholder engagement, and position the organisation as a global thought leader at the intersection of Africa, innovation and storytelling.
	This role combines:
	
		Strategic communications.
		Reputation and narrative management.
		Media and stakeholder engagement.
		Leadership positioning.
		Advanced digital marketing and audience growth.
		You will work directly with senior leadership and across programme, policy, and creative teams to ensure our communications are coherent, compelling, data-driven, and globally competitive.
	
	


What You&rsquo;ll Do
Strategic Communications &amp; Reputation (50%):


	Develop and lead a pan-African and global communications strategy that advances RISE&rsquo; mission and institutional priorities.
	Shape and manage the organisation&rsquo;s public narrative, ensuring clarity, consistency, and influence across platforms.
	Lead reputation management and crisis communications, including risk monitoring and rapid, values-driven responses.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with media, editors, cultural critics, and global platforms.
	Oversee leadership communications including speeches, op-eds, interviews, keynote positioning and international engagements.
	Support the positioning of Rise leadership in global policy, media, and creative ecosystems.
	Lead communications for major projects, including films, campaigns, and high-level convenings.
	Coordinate with external agencies, PR firms, and creative partners to deliver world-class outputs.


Digital Strategy &amp; Audience Growth (50%):


	Design and implement along with a Media Team, a digital-first communications and growth strategy across audiences.
	Lead integrated digital campaigns spanning social media, web, partnerships, and media platforms.
	Oversee RISE&rsquo; digital presence, ensuring content is culturally relevant, globally competitive, and aligned with brand identity.
	Drive audience growth using data analytics, SEO/SEM, CRM tools, and paid media strategies.
	Lead social media strategy and execution - content planning, storytelling, community engagement, and performance tracking.
	Use data insights, social listening, and trend analysis to refine messaging and identify storytelling opportunities.
	Translate complex work in AI, policy, education, and film into compelling, human-centered narratives.
	Build and manage relationships with digital creators, influencers, and media platforms to expand reach and impact.
	Champion a digital-first culture across the organisation, mentoring teams on best practices.


Collaboration &amp; Influence:


	Work closely with Programme, Policy, and Partnerships teams to ensure alignment between communications and impact delivery.
	Serve as a key advisor to leadership on narrative strategy, positioning, and visibility.
	Engage with partners and collaborators to align messaging and amplify shared goals.
	Contribute to positioning RISE within global conversations on Africa, AI, youth, and the creative economy.


Required Skills &amp; Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s or Master&rsquo;s degree in Communications, Media, Marketing, Public Affairs, or a related field
	4-6years of experience in strategic communications, digital marketing, media, or public affairs
	Strong understanding of media landscapes and global communications dynamics
	Proven experience in high-impact, multi-channel campaigns


Demonstrated expertise in:


	Reputation management.
	Media relations and stakeholder engagement.
	Leadership communications.
	Digital strategy and analytics.
	Experience working within development, media, philanthropy, tech, or creative industries is highly desirable.
	Strong networks across media, digital ecosystems, and cultural platforms.
	Exceptional storytelling and writing skills.


What Sets You Apart:


	You think in narratives, not just content.
	You understand how to position RISE within global conversations, not as a participant, but as a leader.
	You are both strategic and hands-on.
	You are comfortable operating in high-visibility, high-stakes environments.
	You bring a deep appreciation for culture, storytelling, innovation, and impact.


Other Requirements:


	Ability to manage multiple high-level projects simultaneously.
	High emotional intelligence and cultural awareness.
	Willingness to travel (domestic and international) as required.


Benefits
Why Join Us:


	At Rise Networks, you will not just be communicating impact, you will be shaping the narrative of a generation.


This is an opportunity to:


	Work at the intersection of Africa, technology, storytelling, and global influence.
	Build and amplify one of the continent&rsquo;s most important platforms for youth and innovation.
	Contribute to work that spans policy, film, AI, and social transformation.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their authentic, brief but concise CV to:&nbsp;people@risenetworks.org&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note


	Only qualified candidates will be contacted.
	This is a full-time, on site position, not a Hybrid, not a remote role.&nbsp;

Communications & Digital Strategy Analyst Job at Rise Networks




        
    

        
        Rise Networks is Nigeria’s pioneering AI for Development (AI4D) organization, building Africa’s next generation of ethical AI talent and innovators. Through research, training, advisory, and policy engagement, we equip individuals and organizations with future – ready skills that bridge learning and employment. Our work advances Tech Justice and Digital Rights, promotes inclusive digital literacy, and drives policy influence and community transformation. By doing so, Rise Networks positions Africa as a co-creator in the global AI ecosystem, shaping an ethical, innovation-driven future.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Communications & Digital Strategy Analyst

		Strategic communications.
		Reputation and narrative management.
		Media and stakeholder engagement.
		Leadership positioning.
		Advanced digital marketing and audience growth.
		You will work directly with senior leadership and across programme, policy, and creative teams to ensure our communications are coherent, compelling, data-driven, and globally competitive.

	Develop and lead a pan-African and global communications strategy that advances RISE’ mission and institutional priorities.
	Shape and manage the organisation’s public narrative, ensuring clarity, consistency, and influence across platforms.
	Lead reputation management and crisis communications, including risk monitoring and rapid, values-driven responses.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with media, editors, cultural critics, and global platforms.
	Oversee leadership communications including speeches, op-eds, interviews, keynote positioning and international engagements.
	Support the positioning of Rise leadership in global policy, media, and creative ecosystems.
	Lead communications for major projects, including films, campaigns, and high-level convenings.
	Coordinate with external agencies, PR firms, and creative partners to deliver world-class outputs.


Digital Strategy & Audience Growth (50%):


	Design and implement along with a Media Team, a digital-first communications and growth strategy across audiences.
	Lead integrated digital campaigns spanning social media, web, partnerships, and media platforms.
	Oversee RISE’ digital presence, ensuring content is culturally relevant, globally competitive, and aligned with brand identity.
	Drive audience growth using data analytics, SEO/SEM, CRM tools, and paid media strategies.
	Lead social media strategy and execution - content planning, storytelling, community engagement, and performance tracking.
	Use data insights, social listening, and trend analysis to refine messaging and identify storytelling opportunities.
	Translate complex work in AI, policy, education, and film into compelling, human-centered narratives.
	Build and manage relationships with digital creators, influencers, and media platforms to expand reach and impact.
	Champion a digital-first culture across the organisation, mentoring teams on best practices.

	Work closely with Programme, Policy, and Partnerships teams to ensure alignment between communications and impact delivery.

	Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Communications, Media, Marketing, Public Affairs, or a related field

	You think in narratives, not just content.
	You understand how to position RISE within global conversations, not as a participant, but as a leader.
	You are both strategic and hands-on.

	Ability to manage multiple high-level projects simultaneously.

	Proven track record of achieving sales targets and managing large territories.
	Experience in Cement or paint sales will be an added advantage
	Experience in B2B and project sales is essential.

Sales Strategy & Execution:


	Develop and implement strategic sales plans to achieve company targets.
	Identify new business opportunities in construction, real estate, and infrastructure sectors.
	Drive product penetration across retail, wholesale, and project channels.


Business Development:


	Build and maintain relationships with key stakeholders including contractors, distributors, developers, and retailers.
	Identify and secure high-value projects requiring cement and screeding materials.
	Monitor market trends, competitor activity, and pricing strategies.


Team Leadership:


	Lead, mentor, and manage the sales team to achieve performance targets.
	Set KPIs and conduct regular performance reviews.
	Provide training on product knowledge and sales techniques.


Channel Management:


	Expand and manage distributor and dealer networks.
	Ensure product availability and visibility across key markets.
	Optimize route-to-market strategies.


Revenue & Performance Management:


	Achieve sales volume, revenue, and profitability targets.
	Prepare sales forecasts, budgets, and reports.
	Track performance metrics and adjust strategies as needed.


Customer Relationship Management:


	Maintain strong relationships with key accounts.
	Resolve customer issues and ensure high levels of satisfaction.
	Negotiate contracts and pricing agreements.


Key Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Engineering, or related fields.
	MBA is an added advantage.


Experience:


	5–12 years of sales experience, preferably in FMCG or building materials (cement, screeding, construction products).
	Proven track record of achieving sales targets and managing large territories.
	Experience in Cement or paint sales will be an added advantage
	Experience in B2B and project sales is essential.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Quality Control Manager Job at Afconrecruit Limited




        
    

        
        Afconrecruit Limited - Our client, a food manufacturing company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: FMCG/Foods/Beverage

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The successful candidate will oversee the entire Quality Control (QC), Quality Assurance (QA), R&D, and New Product Development (NPD) departments, as well as supervise Production and the training of staff on quality parameters.
	The manager must independently handle audits such as NAFDAC, SON, HACCP, ISO 22000, and other relevant standards. Key responsibilities also include people management and a willingness to take on additional duties, such as Store and Dispatch management, as required.


Requirements


	Education: Bachelor’s Degree / HND in Food Science, Food Technology, Microbiology, Biochemistry, or a related field.
	Experience: 3–4 years of post-qualification experience specifically in the Food & Beverage manufacturing sector.
	Certifications: Professional certification in HACCP (Level 3 or higher), ISO 22000 (FSMS), or Quality Management Systems (QMS) is highly desirable.
	Willing to resume immediately.
